[2018-07-09 12:34] supergreyman (Greyman): Construct a prompt Plexo Cell, and construct a Plexo Cell with :Exhaust: 1
[2018-07-09 12:35] supergreyman (Greyman): Just bump the HP to 4 since it's pretty weak.
[2018-07-09 12:36] supergreyman (Greyman): Or you can construct a prompt Doomed Wall.
[2018-07-09 12:36] f300xen (f0x): the problem with constructing units is that you soak with them
[2018-07-09 12:37] supergreyman (Greyman): Yes, but it should be somehow buffed anyway.
[2018-07-09 12:37] f300xen (f0x): constructing a plexo every turn is worth like 8 drones
[2018-07-09 12:44] supergreyman (Greyman): The best way to do this is to make it a spell, like "Construct 2 Immolites, and construct a Wall with :Exhaust: 1."
[2018-07-09 12:46] supergreyman (Greyman): And you can probably guess it's a bit more flexible and lets you soak with Wall, which isn't avoidable.
[2018-07-09 12:47] supergreyman (Greyman): But I rather have a flexible unit that lets you do more things than a stiff unit.
[2018-07-09 12:48] supergreyman (Greyman): Or you can simply give this Lumina Guardian a Start of turn Iso Kronus ability.
[2018-07-09 12:48] supergreyman (Greyman): That's probably the simplest solution.
[2018-07-09 12:50] supergreyman (Greyman): I think that's a bit better if you don't want it to be flexible like the spell idea.

[2018-07-10 02:12] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Ok, the units I keep coming up with for a "must click all" mechanic aren't very interesting.  If it's an attacker+blocker, you can hold the first 1-2 but after that you would just prefer to always click.  If it's nonfragile defender+econ it seems to always go one way.  The ones that stand out as having potential are fragile blocker+econ and no finesse pixies.
Lattice Drone (3 :Gold: :Green: :Energy: , 10 Supply) 3 :Fragile: 
:Blocker: , 🐜 Hivemind: All units of the same name must be clicked together.
:Click: : gain :Gold: 

Black Market Dynamite (1 :Gold: :Green: :Blue: , 10 Supply) 2 :Health: 
🐜 Hivemind: All units of the same name must be clicked together.
:Click: and sacrifice Discount Dynamite: gain :Attack: :Attack: 

Special Shipment (4 :Gold: :Green: :Blue: :Blue: , 4 Supply) Spell
Construct 3 Black Market Dynamite with :Time: 2

[2018-07-10 13:21] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): Frosted Relay (14 :Blue: :Red: :Red:, supply 4) 3:Health: :Blocker: :Lifespan:5
As an additional cost to buy Frosted Relay, :Exhaust:1 two of your blocking units with 3 or more health
Start of turn: Gain :Attack: :Attack: and construct a Frostbite with :Lifespan:1
[2018-07-10 13:23] cachotognax: I think it's a bit too strong, it has an added cost of 10 :Blue: :Blue: true but you pay it only once, you can then buy as many as you want and once you stop you also have 2 extra walls.
[2018-07-10 13:24] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): Actually I think "As an additional cost to buy Frosted Relay, :Exhaust:1 two of your blocking units with 3 or more health" makes more sense for the additional cost
[2018-07-10 13:26] cachotognax: wait it also absorb 3 while attacking? that thing is stronk.
[2018-07-10 13:28] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): you are basically paying 6 defense for the unit
[2018-07-10 13:31] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): 3 attack and 3 delayed chill (the frostbites are still BT2) seems like it would be worth a breach though, so I reduced the attack
[2018-07-10 13:33] supergreyman (Greyman): The way to play it is to buy Frosted Relays, then buy good defender.
[2018-07-10 13:34] supergreyman (Greyman): Also, there's no way you can target 2 units from one ability.
[2018-07-10 13:37] supergreyman (Greyman): And because it's so easy to mitigate the self-freeze effect, 10BR is too cheap for 2 :Attack:, vigilant 4 :Health:, and a free Frostbite every turn. Like, it even makes Tatsu look bad.
[2018-07-10 13:40] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): nerfed it but didn't increase the self-freeze effect
[2018-07-10 13:42] supergreyman (Greyman): I think the purchase effect would look better if it can create freeze units for your opponent, like 2 Nivo Charges for your opponent.
[2018-07-10 13:42] supergreyman (Greyman): It would look like a unit that can more easily implemented instead of something entirely new.
[2018-07-10 13:42] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): might as well give the unit BT2 then
[2018-07-10 13:43] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): so it actually hurts your defense like it was intended to
[2018-07-10 13:45] supergreyman (Greyman): The one thing about self-freeze is that your opponent can only take advantage of it if he has some attackers and you have some defenders. It would not happen if the unit can be bought in the early game (thus 10BR is too cheap since it can be rushed).
[2018-07-10 13:46] supergreyman (Greyman): You can price it so that it can't be bought too early.
[2018-07-10 13:46] supergreyman (Greyman): Or you can have a cheaper price, but add buildtime to it.
[2018-07-10 13:47] supergreyman (Greyman): Actually, that wouldn't work since the freeze ability will kick in once you purchase jt anyway.
[2018-07-10 13:50] supergreyman (Greyman): Not sure if there's a need for Lifespan.
[2018-07-10 13:51] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): I don't think BT2 is necessary if the Nivo Charge on purchase effect is used, and 14BRR seems better for it
[2018-07-10 13:55] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): the one thing I'm not too keen about the Nivo Charge on purchase effect is that it no longer requires you to actually have two blockers with 3HP or higher
[2018-07-10 13:56] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): it doesn't feel like you're paying defense
[2018-07-10 13:57] supergreyman (Greyman): If your Wall is exhausted, it can no longer block for 1 turn. If your Wall is frozen, it can no longer block for 1 turn too.
[2018-07-10 13:57] darkeisbein: I think the constructing frostbite thing is inellegant as opposed to just giving it a click to freeze
[2018-07-10 13:58] darkeisbein: also the cost is really inellegant from an UI point of view
[2018-07-10 13:58] darkeisbein: like how do you select the units to sacrifice?
[2018-07-10 13:58] darkeisbein: however you do it it's gonna be clunky
[2018-07-10 13:58] darkeisbein: realistically if you want it to have that cost just make it cost wall
[2018-07-10 13:58] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, it's just that it can't be vigilant if you make it a click effect.
[2018-07-10 13:59] darkeisbein: then don't try to make it vigilant
[2018-07-10 13:59] supergreyman (Greyman): ^ I would suggest that too.
[2018-07-10 14:00] darkeisbein: I mean vigilant attack + threat of freeze is still plenty vigilant anyways
[2018-07-10 14:01] supergreyman (Greyman): I would get rid of the Lifespan too. It kind of makes it look weak.
[2018-07-10 14:02] supergreyman (Greyman): Unless you really want it to be a temporary attacker.
[2018-07-10 14:02] supergreyman (Greyman): But that would look strange to me.

Click: :Chill:  5

[2018-07-10 14:04] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): kind of defeats the whole freaking concept does it?
[2018-07-10 14:04] supergreyman (Greyman): You can't consume prompt Walls BTW.
[2018-07-10 14:05] darkeisbein: the unit is probably still overcosted like crazy
[2018-07-10 14:05] darkeisbein: like 10BB * r + 5 essentially
[2018-07-10 14:06] supergreyman (Greyman): Other than the price, it functions exactly like Tatsu.

[2018-07-10 14:06] darkeisbein: the idea is that it is a great unit in  freeze poke situations

[2018-07-11 20:06] kaelii: The other awkward thing about the design is while the Gauss Explosive has lifespan, Gaussite does not decay at end of turn so if you aren't sure you'll pop them efficiently in the next few turns you should probably just save the green until you ARE pretty sure you want to pop them.
[2018-07-11 20:08] kaelii: Like its an awkward threat source because you're turning a flexible resource that has no real incentive to be spent right now unless it does something, into something you only really want to have going into the turn you plan to use it.

[2018-07-12 04:10] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): hmm, how is exhaust+lifespan borked?
[2018-07-12 04:13] namington: lifespan doesnt tick down when exhausted
[2018-07-12 04:13] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): attack>gold might be more of a probably a bad idea like a SDR violation, rather than a borked mess like FoF
[2018-07-12 04:13] namington: converting gold to attack violates proper order
[2018-07-12 04:14] namington: theres a blog post about it somewhere but basically
[2018-07-12 04:14] namington: certain conversions are only allowed to work one-way
[2018-07-12 04:14] namington: in this case, attack can only be converted immediately to gold, not vice versa
[2018-07-12 04:14] namington: the reason for this is the undo mechanic
[2018-07-12 04:15] namington: it's possible that, if you make a unit that changes the order, there might be a gamestate that can only be achieved by utilizing undo
[2018-07-12 04:15] namington: it's even possible it creates an infinite resource loop if the exact right units are involved

[2018-07-12 15:21] darkeisbein: probably a bit to efficient
[2018-07-12 15:22] darkeisbein: like it is essentially giving more then 1 attack since your opponents attack arrives later then yours
[2018-07-12 15:22] darkeisbein: assuming that it even does anything for your opponent
[2018-07-12 15:22] darkeisbein: on top of that it is also bp potentially leading to crazy bp rushes
[2018-07-12 15:24] darkeisbein: still crazy rush unit
[2018-07-12 15:24] supergreyman (Greyman): I was thinking that it's slightly more efficient than a Steelsplitter to justify.
[2018-07-12 15:24] darkeisbein: if feel like it is much more efficient then a splitter
[2018-07-12 15:25] darkeisbein: like essentially you gain two splitters but give you opponent a bt2 goose
[2018-07-12 15:25] supergreyman (Greyman): Or rather a Tarsier.
[2018-07-12 15:26] darkeisbein: and this prolly also increases inflation with how it gives both players attack
[2018-07-12 15:26] darkeisbein: so the opponent getting attack half a turn later is even more pronounced
[2018-07-12 15:27] supergreyman (Greyman): That's a good point.
[2018-07-12 15:28] darkeisbein: and on top of that you also get a value hold#
[2018-07-12 15:37] leary93: its a bt1 goose for opp
[2018-07-12 15:37] leary93: because the charge hits right after you click
[2018-07-12 15:37] leary93: it looks fine to me, it's not shocky for 2 tech
[2018-07-12 15:37] leary93: but maybe lower the hp?
[2018-07-12 15:37] leary93: its a bit too compareable to sentry
[2018-07-12 15:38] darkeisbein: well the point is that your opponent get's it half a turn after you
[2018-07-12 15:38] darkeisbein: so you get two attackers and your opponent get's one half a turn later
[2018-07-12 15:38] leary93: which isn't that big of a difference, because half-turn inflation isnt that high

[2018-07-12 18:05] swapgo: Well, it seems like a very expensive, non-blocking, squishier Shredder than your opponent can play around by using the secret techniques of being efficient and not overteching G
[2018-07-12 18:10] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Will making it cheaper and able to block make it more interesting?
[2018-07-12 18:14] swapgo: Problem is that draining resources is definitely a new area and should probably be done with an unit that can already stand on its feet
[2018-07-12 18:16] swapgo: It's also a touchy area because SDR is a thing
[2018-07-12 18:16] cachotognax: draining resources could only be usefull if it's more valuable than dealing damage, so if it's the early game/there's good soak in the set it might make sense.
[2018-07-12 18:16] swapgo: You couldn't make an unit that, say, steals resources or punishes decay because then you'd make a corner case where having something is worse than not having it
[2018-07-12 18:17] swapgo: Also yeah what Cacho said. Usually doing damage is doing you more favors than destroying resources
[2018-07-12 18:17] swapgo: 1 unblocked attack usually destroys 2 Gold or so
